g.data-narration: Welcome! Today we explore mkdir() and rmdir() - fundamental UNIX system calls for directory management.
g.text: mkdir() and rmdir() Functions in UNIX/Linux
Essential System Calls for Directory Management

g.data-narration: mkdir() creates new empty directories while rmdir() removes empty directories. Both are atomic operations.
g.text: • mkdir() - Creates a new empty directory with specified pathname and permissions
• rmdir() - Removes an empty directory from the file system
• Both are atomic system calls - either complete entirely or fail completely

g.data-narration: These functions are essential for file system management in system programming, applications, and shell scripting.
g.text: • Foundation of file system operations in UNIX-like operating systems
• Critical for system programming, application development, and shell scripting
• Demonstrate key OS concepts: system calls, kernel interaction, error handling

g.data-narration: mkdir() requires sys/stat.h header. It takes a pathname and mode (permissions) as parameters, returns 0 on success.
g.text: Prototype: int mkdir(const char *pathname, mode_t mode);

g.data-narration: The pathname parameter specifies the directory to create. The mode parameter sets permissions like 0755 for rwxr-xr-x.
rmdir() requires unistd.h header. It takes only pathname, returns 0 on success. Key: directory must be empty!
rmdir() takes pathname only. It removes empty directories. Returns -1 on error with errno set appropriately.
g.text: pathname: Path of directory to remove

g.data-narration: errno values help diagnose failures. EEXIST means path exists, EACCES means permission denied, ENOENT means path not found.
Final permissions = mode & ~umask. With umask 022 and mode 0777, final permissions become 0755 (rwxr-xr-x).
g.text: rwxr-xr-x (Owner: rwx, Group: r-x, Other: r-x)

g.data-narration: Every new directory automatically contains '.' (self-reference) and '..' (parent directory) entries.
This example shows creating a simple directory with error handling using strerror() to display human-readable error messages.
Creating nested directories requires a loop to create each component. Handle EEXIST error to skip existing directories.

text.tspan: // Parse path and create each component

g.data-narration: Safe rmdir checks path exists, verifies it's a directory, then attempts removal with proper error messages.
Key takeaways: mkdir uses sys/stat.h, rmdir uses unistd.h. Both return 0 on success, -1 on failure. rmdir requires empty directories.
g.text: Both return 0 on success, -1 on error | rmdir() only removes empty directories | final_perms = mode & ~umask

g.data-narration: Exam tips: Memorize headers, understand umask formula, remember rmdir requires empty directories, know common errno values.
g.text: 1. mkdir() = sys/stat.h, rmdir() = unistd.h | 2. Remember umask formula | 3. rmdir requires empty dir | 4. Both return 0/-1 | 5. '.' and '..' auto-created
